content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 28, 1994

Frank St. Denis
Chairman
Citizens For All Water Options
900 Embarcadero
Morro Bay, California 93442

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-94-286

Dear Mr. St. Denis:

We respond to your September 2, 1994 request for advice
regarding the allowable uses of campaign funds by a local ballot
measure committee under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

May a local ballot measure committee contribute its funds to
local politicians who supported the committee's position in the
election?

CONCLUSION

As long as the expenditure does not confer a personal benefit
on any individual with the authority to approve the expenditure,
committee funds may be used for any expenditure reasonably related
to a political purpose. Contributions to candidates who share the
same view on the ballot measure issue supported by your committee
would be an expenditure which is reasonably related to the
political purpose of the committee.

FACTS

Citizens For All Water Options is a recipient committee for a
measure in a special election held August 9, 1994. The committee
wishes to terminate its existence and dispose of its remaining
funds. The committee would like to contribute its funds to three
candidates running for city council in the November election. The

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5860

File No. A-94-286
Page 2


three candidates supported the committee's position in the special
election.

ANALYSIS

Section 89512.5 controls the use of funds by a committee not
controlled by a candidate. Generally, any committee expenditure
reasonably related to a political purpose is permitted. If an
expenditure confers a substantial personal benefit on an
individual who has authority to approve an expenditure of campaign
funds held by the committee, then such an expenditure must be
directly related to the political purpose of the committee.

The contribution of a local ballot measure committee's funds
to local candidates who share the committee's political agenda
appears to be directly related to the political purposes of the
committee, especially where, as here, the ballot measure election
has already been held. Accordingly, this expenditure of committee
funds is permitted under the Act.²

We trust that this letter fully addresses your request for
advice. Should you have any questions please contact the
undersigned at (916) 322-5901.


Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Daniel E. Muallem
Counsel, Legal Division


2 We have not addressed any state or federal taxation issues that
may arise from such an expenditure. You may wish to contact the
Franchise Tax Board and Internal Revenue Service for advice.
